summary: Add the Dashboard and Opportunity List UI slice with Top 5 opportunities, score-sorted list, filters, reusable OpportunityCard and ScoreBadge components, and focused tests.
acceptance_criteria: A /dashboard page exists and displays today/top opportunities sorted by totalScore descending, including a Top 5 section and summary metrics derived from persisted Opportunity/SearchRun data.
A /opportunities page exists and displays a score-sorted opportunity list with query-string filters for radar task, minimum score, tool type, risk level, and status where data exists.

Reusable OpportunityCard and ScoreBadge components exist under components/ and render score, keyword/title, tool type, market/language, summary, monetization/risk cues, and a detail link target for each opportunity.
A server-side data helper exists at lib/opportunities.ts to query opportunities and dashboard data through Prisma without duplicating UI query logic; it returns plain serializable view models.
The UI uses restrained operational styling consistent with the existing app, remains responsive, avoids nested cards, and does not add marketing hero content or decorative visual noise.
Focused automated tests cover opportunity sorting, Top 5 selection, filter behavior, view-model serialization, score badge/risk rendering where practical, and empty states.
README documents the Dashboard/Opportunity List slice, local data dependency, filter query parameters, validation commands, and explicit non-goals.

No Opportunity Detail implementation, Save/Discard/Build Next mutation, RunScanButton wiring, RecentRunStatus component, API route, scan orchestration, database schema migration, auth, cron, deployment, package dependency, real provider, or agent change is implemented.
notes: Derived from docs/09_codex_implementation_tasks.md Task 12 and docs/11_development_plan.md M5.

docs/09 says clicking enters detail, but Task 13 owns /opportunities/[id] details. This task may render stable links to /opportunities/[id] but must not implement the detail page or status mutations unless separately tasked.
systems_architect is required because this introduces shared UI/data-access boundaries over persisted scan/opportunity records.
domain_designer is required because this is the main ranking/list presentation surface and must not overstate mock scan output or hide risk semantics.
non_goals: No /opportunities/[id] detail page, score breakdown detail view, SERP weakness detail, MVP Spec, Copy MVP Spec, Save/Discard/Build Next mutation, or status update endpoint.
No RunScanButton behavior, RecentRunStatus component, Radar Task detail page, dashboard scan trigger, cron, background jobs, or API route changes.
No Prisma schema changes, migrations, seed changes, package changes, provider setup, LLM setup, env secret loading, auth/session, deployment, or GitHub Actions.
No changes to agents, services, scan orchestrator, scoring formula, Radar Task CRUD API, app shell, global styles, or landing page unless required only for import paths.
